title: eusteralis tomentosa
intro: Eusteralis tomentosa, also known as the Australian peppermint, is a shrubby plant that belongs to the Rutaceae family and is native to Australia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Eusteralis tomentosa include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. The leaves of the plant can be used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
